title: Analytics: the Overview dashboard

publish.description: Read the Analytics Overview — headline metric cards, the date range, and the per-collection performance table with CTR trend and uplift.

intro: The Analytics section shows what qSortBy is doing for your store. Its Overview tab rolls everything up: headline metrics at the top and a per-collection breakdown below. This guide walks through the cards, the date range, and the performance table.

steps.heading: Read the headline metrics
steps.body: Open **Analytics** from the left navigation. The Overview opens on four metric cards: **Collections managed**, **Avg. collection CTR**, **Auto-sorts run**, and **Revenue influenced**. Each shows its current value plus the change versus the previous period, so you can see the trend at a glance.

els.text: Open Analytics from the left menu — the four cards summarize collections managed, average CTR, auto-sorts run, and revenue influenced.

notes.text: These are mocked preview numbers — your store's real figures appear once qSortBy is installed and collecting data.

steps.heading: Set the date range
steps.body: Every number on the page is scoped to a **date range** — shown here as **Last 30 days**. Change it to compare a busy launch week against a quieter month, or to check performance over a longer window. The cards and the table below both update together.

els.text: Last 30 days sets the window for every metric and every row in the table.

steps.heading: Compare collections in the table
steps.body: **Performance by collection** breaks the totals down row by row. Each collection shows its live signal, **CTR (30d)** with a sparkline **CTR trend**, views, revenue, and an **Uplift** figure — how much better it's doing since qSortBy took over sorting. Click any column header to sort by it.

els.text: Each row shows CTR, trend, revenue and Uplift — the gain since qSortBy started sorting. Click a header to re-sort.

outro: That's the Analytics Overview: four headline cards, a date range that scopes the whole view, and a per-collection table where Uplift shows exactly what qSortBy's sorting is adding. Use the tabs at the top to dig into Customer events and the live Event log.
